draft.introduction: Proverbs 1 opens the wisdom collection attributed to Solomon, personifying Wisdom as a woman who cries aloud in the public square. The fear of the Lord is declared the beginning of knowledge.

translation.text: The proverbs of Solomon, son of David, king of Israel: to know wisdom and instruction, to understand words of insight. The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge; fools despise wisdom and instruction. Wisdom cries aloud in the street; in the markets she raises her voice: ‘How long, O simple ones, will you love being simple?’
translation.original_text: Παροιμίαι Σαλωμῶντος υἱοῦ Δαυειδ ὃς ἐβασίλευσεν ἐν Ισραηλ

translator_notes.note: The “fear of the Lord” (φόβος Κυρίου) is not terror but reverential awe. It functions as the epistemological foundation of the entire wisdom tradition: right relationship with God is the prerequisite for understanding reality.
